structuralHomes.definition: Application composition or framework-level app home for route, layout, host, or entry structures.
Static asset or shipped resource home.
Executable command or package-bin surface home.
Repository-owned document-engine package root home.
Repository-owned validator package root home.
Compatibility, shim, adapter, or transition-support home.
Configuration and environment-oriented setup home.
Structured payload, registry-data, fixture-data, or reusable data home.
Human-facing documentation home.
Human-facing documentation home using plural docs convention.
Demo, tutorial, sample, or demonstrative material home.
Prototype, spike, playground, or relaxed-rule exploratory home.
Machine-generated output home where generated status is structurally important.
Operations, infrastructure, deployment, monitoring, or CI/CD home.
Public static asset or framework public resource home.
Tooling automation home for scripts, CLIs, migrations, codegen, or repository tasks.
Primary source or implementation home for shipped or executed code.
Quality-support home using singular test convention.
Quality-support home for tests, fixtures, mocks, benchmarks, or validation artifacts.
Repository tooling, support utility, or developer-tool home.
Third-party, vendored, or externally maintained material home.
